rule_text: Headlines must be sentence case (capitalize only the first word and proper nouns). Example: 'Register for spring break activities' not 'Register for Spring Break Activities'.
Headlines should be active and complete, having both a subject and verb.
Use the Explorer brand archetype: curious, bold, authentic and focused on discovery and growth. Write in a tone that feels energetic and student-friendly while remaining professional.
Write in third person but 'you' and 'your' are acceptable for student-facing content. Avoid 'I', 'we', 'us' and 'our'.
Items should be of general student interest and open to all students. Items intended for a small subset of students should be handled through other means.
Student recitals are not included in My UI.
Items must be unique and not duplicated across Weekly Events, News and Updates, or Highlighted Events sections.
My UI items rotate weekly with different events featured in Highlighted Events each week. Submissions are curated based on timeliness.
Events with an RSVP deadline may run once with the deadline and again close to the event. Events without RSVP run in reasonable proximity to the event date.
Weekly Events cover Monday through the following Sunday. Events are organized chronologically by day. Include dates for events outside the publication cycle week.
Weekly events are edited for accuracy and formatting only. Titles and descriptions should not be rewritten unless correcting an error.
Each News and Updates item should be one paragraph with a headline. No bullet points. Headlines must be active and complete with a clear subject and verb.
Calendar items are linked to the permalink address, not the entire calendar.
Events outside Moscow must indicate location. Events in Moscow but off campus must include the street address but not the city. Items are Pacific time unless otherwise noted.
Highlighted events need a photo or art that can be cropped to 600x600 pixel square.
Academic Dates and Deadlines section should include 30 days of upcoming information from the U of I academic calendar.
Student job postings are listed for two weeks. Off-campus jobs link to VandalWeb Career Center page.
